attributes: appeared in this world with Nityānanda Prabhu
surrounded by His devoted followers
appeared in this world with Śrī Caitanya Mahāprabhu

1.1.verse_text: I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of my spiritual master. I offer my respectful obeisances to Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu, who appeared in this world with Nityananda Prabhu and is surrounded by His devotees.

attributes: possessor of two lotus feet worshipped with respectful obeisances
son of Nanda Maharaj surrounded by the gopīs
enchants the minds of all the residents of Vṛndāvana

1.2.verse_text: I offer my respectful obeisances to the two lotus feet Srimati Radharani. I offer my respectful obeisances Lord Nandanandana, who is surrounded by the gopis and enchants the minds of the residents of Vrndavana.

attributes: abode of the celebrated King and Queen of Vrndavana
celebrated Queen of Vrndavana whose associates are described in Vedic literatures
celebrated King of Vrndavana whose associates are described in Vedic literatures

1.3.verse_text: The celebrated personal associates of the King and Queen of Vrndavana are briefly but truthfully described with great delight in both Vedic literatures and oral tradition. This book will also describe them.

1.4-5.verse_text: These associates of the Lord are described by the residents of Mathura-mandala, by the various books written by devotees, various Vedic literatures such as the Puranas and Agamas and by the great devotees and saintly persons. For the satisfaction of my dear friends, these associates of the Lord will now be briefly describing in writing, following the previous authorities in the path of ecstatic love of God.

attributes: has associates residing in the land of Vrajabhūmi
residence for the associates of Śrī Kṛṣṇa

1.6.verse_text: The associates of Krsna who reside in Vrajabhumi may be divided into three groups : 1. protectors of domestic animals, 2. brahmanas, 3. others.

attributes: dynasty of protectors of domestic animals divided into three groups

1.7.verse_text: The Protectors of Domestic Animals: These are descendants of the Yadu dynasty and they may be divided into three groups : 1. vaisyas, 2. abhiras, 3. gurjaras.

attributes: important vaisyas who earn their livelihood by protecting cows
some vaisyas are born from lower-caste mothers
some vaisyas are known as abhiras

1.8.verse_text: The Vaisyas: For the most part the most important vaisyas earn their livelihood by protecting cows. Some of the vaisyas are born from lower-caste mothers and they are known as abhiras.

attributes: descendants of Vaiśya fathers and Śūdra mothers
engaged in the livelihood of protecting buffalo herds
considered slightly inferior to the cow-protecting Vaiśyas
known by various names including the name ghosa

1.9.verse_text: The Abhiras: The abhiras are those who have vaisya fathers and sudra mothers. A little inferior to the vaisyas, who are engaged in cow protection, the abhiras earn their livelihood by protecting buffalo. They are known by various names, such as ghosa.

attributes: a group of people considered a little inferior to the Abhiras
earn their livelihood by protecting goats and similar animals
possess bodily features that are a little chubby
reside on the outskirts of Vraja
sacred land on whose outskirts the Garjaras reside

1.10.verse_text: The Garjaras: A little inferior to the abhiras, the garjaras earn their livelihood by protecting goats and similar animals. Their bodily features are a little chubby and they reside on the outskirts of Vraja.

attributes: learned in the knowledge of all the Vedas
engage in performing sacrifices for the pleasure of the Lord
dedicated to worshiping the Deity according to scriptural injunctions
faithfully occupy themselves with traditional brahminical occupations

1.11.verse_text: The Brahmanas: Learned in all the Vedas, the brahmanas engage in performing sacrifices, worshiping the Deity and other brahminical occupations.

attributes: earn their livelihood by engaging in various trades and crafts
identified as one of the five kinds of associates of Lord Hari
Lord Hari residing in the land of Vrajabhumi
residence of Lord Hari and His five kinds of associates

1.12.verse_text: The Bahisthas: The bahisthas earn their livelihood by engaging in various trades and crafts. In this way we have described five kinds of associates (vaisyas, abhiras, garjaras, brahmanas and bahisthas) of Lord Hari in Vrajabhumi.

attributes: always respectful to His worshipable superiors
supported by eight groups of associates including superiors and friends
has brothers and relatives who are worshipable superiors to Kṛṣṇa
has contemporary friends who are worshipable superiors to Kṛṣṇa

1.13.verse_text: Lord Krsna's associates may also be divided into the following eight groups : 1. worshipable superiors, 2. relatives who are on the same level (such as brothers and sisters) 3. gopi messengers, 4. servants, 5. craftsmen, 6. maidservants, 7. contemporary cowherd friends and 8. dear gopi friends. Included among the worshipable superiors are the brothers and similar relatives of Nanda Maharaja, Nanda's contemporary friends, servants, other associates and the elderly gopis. Krsna is always respectful to these superiors.

attributes: grandson of the elderly relative serving as grandfather
possessor of worshipable superiors including elderly relatives and brahmanas

1.14.verse_text: 1. Worshipable Superiors: The worshipable superiors include Krnsa's grandfather, other elderly relatives and also the community of brahmanas.

attributes: fair-complexioned grandfather of Kṛṣṇa with white hair and white attire
like a great cloud showering the nectar of auspiciousness
best of all the residents of Vraja
worshipped Lord Nārāyaṇa at Nandisvara-pura following Devarsi Narada's advice
grandson of Parjanya Mahārāja
sage whose advice was followed by Parjanya Mahārāja
husband of Lakṣmī who was worshipped by Parjanya Mahārāja
site where Parjanya Mahārāja worshipped Lord Nārāyaṇa

1.15-16.verse_text: Krsna's fair-complexion, white-haired and white-attired grandfather is named Parjanya Maharaja, because he is like a great cloud (parjanya) showering the nectar of auspiciousness. He is the best of all the residents of Vraja. Desiring a worthy descendant, he followed the advice of Devarsi Narada and engaged in the worship of Lord Narayana, the husband of Laksmi. As he was worshiping Narayana at Nandisvara-pura, a pleasant voice spoke the following words from the sky.

attributes: the best among the five sons obtained through pious austerities
father of the glorious son who delights the residents of Vraja
glorious son of Nanda who delights the residents of Vraja
possesses power to defeat all oppositions
worshiped by demigods and demons touching jewels of crowns to His lotus feet

1.17-18.verse_text: "Because of your pious austerities you will obtain five sons. Of these sons the middle one, named Nanda, will be the best. Nanda's glorious son will delight the residents of Vraja. He will defeat all oppositions. Both the demigods and demons will worship Him, touching the jewels of their crowns to His lotus feet."

attributes: became happy upon hearing news of the residence
decided to reside at the place until the Kesi demon arrived
became frightened and fled with all his associates to Mahavana
demonic presence whose arrival caused fear to Parjanya Maharaja
place where Parjanya Maharaja fled with all his associates

1.19.verse_text: Hearing this, Parjanya Maharaja became happy and decided to reside at the place. He lived there until he saw the Kesi demon arrive and at that time he became frightened and fled with all his associates to Mahavana.

attributes: grandmother of Kṛṣṇa who is respected in the land of Vraja
short in stature with a complexion the colour of a kusumbha flower
attired in green garments and possesses hair the color of milk
grandson of Variyasī-devī
land where Kṛṣṇa's grandmother is highly respected

1.20.verse_text: Krsna's grandmother Variyasi is respected in the land of Vraja. She is short in stature and her complexion is the colour of a kusumbha flower. She is attired in green garments and her hair is the color of milk.

attributes: has two brothers named Urjanya and Rajanya
has an expert dancer sister named Suverjana
brother of Parjanya Mahārāja
brother of Parjanya Mahārāja

town where Suverjana and Guṇavīra reside

1.21.verse_text: Parjanya Maharaja's brothers are named Urjanya and Rajanya and his sister is an expert dancer named Suverjana. Her husband is named Gunavira and they live in the town named Surya-kunja.

attributes: father of Śrī Kṛṣṇa
delights the residents of Vraja-dhāma
worshiped by all the worlds
has a protuberant belly
complexion is the colour of sandalwood
tall in stature
wears garments the colour of a bandhujīva flower
has a beard like a mixture of rice and toasted sesame seeds
son of Nanda Mahārāja

1.22-23.verse_text: Krsna's father is Nanda Maharaja. Nanda delights the residents of Vraja and he is worshiped by all the worlds. He has a protuberant belly, his complexion is the colour of sandalwood, he is tall in stature and his garments are the colour of a bandhujiva flower. His beard is a mixture of black and white hairs, like rice and toasted sesame seeds mixed together.

attributes: king of the cowherd men in Vrajabhūmi
younger brother of Upananda
intimate friend of Mahārāja Vasudeva
husband of Yaśodā-devī and father of Lord Kṛṣṇa
elder brother of Nanda Mahārāja
intimate friend of Nanda Mahārāja
queen of Vrajabhūmi and wife of Nanda Mahārāja
mother of Lord Kṛṣṇa
son of Nanda Mahārāja and Yaśodā-devī

1.24.verse_text: Nanda is the king of the cowherd men, the younger brother of Upananda and the intimate friend of Maharaja Vasudeva. Nanda and his wife Yasoda are the king and queen of Vrajabhumi and the parents of Lord Krsna.

attributes: possesses the splendor of great wealth
famous by the name Anakadundubhi
known as Vasudeva because he possesses great wealth
named Drona in his previous birth

1.25.verse_text: Because he possesses the splendor (deva) of great wealth (vasu), Nanda Maharaja is also known as Vasudeva. He is also famous by the name Anakadundubhi and it is known that in his previous birth he was named Drona.

attributes: has his names described in the Mathura-mahima section of the Garuda Purana
has Maharaja Vrsabhanu as his most intimate friend
most intimate friend of Nanda Maharaja

1.26.verse_text: These names of Maharaja Nanda are described in the Mathura-mahima section of the Garuda Purana. Nanda's most intimate friend is named Maharaja Vrsabhanu.

attributes: bestows fame upon the cowherd residents of Vraja
personification of parental love for Śrī Kṛṣṇa
possesses a dark complexion
wears garments that resemble a rainbow
object of the parental love personified by Yaśodā-devī

1.27.verse_text: Krnsa's mother is named Yasoda because she bestows (da) fame (yasah) on the cowherd residents of Vraja. She is like the personification of parental love for Krnsa. Her complexion is dark and her garments are like a rainbow.

attributes: body is of a medium size, neither large nor small
possesses beautiful long black hair
one of the closest friends of Mother Yaśodā
one of the closest friends of Mother Yaśodā

1.28.verse_text: Mother Yasoda's body is of a medium size, neither large nor small. She has long black hair. Her closest friends are Aindavi and Kirtida.

attributes: wife of Nanda the king of Gokula
queen of the cowherd men in Vraja
friend of Devaki
mother of Lord Kṛṣṇa
husband of Yaśodā-devī
king of Gokula
son of Yaśodā-devī

1.29.verse_text: Yasoda is the wife of Nanda, the king of Gokula. She is the queen of the cowherd men in Vraja, the friend of Devaki and the mother of Lord Krsna.

attributes: wife of Mahārāja Nanda who was also known as Devaki
great friend of the wife of Mahārāja Vasudeva
husband of Yaśodā-devī
wife of Mahārāja Vasudeva and great friend of Yaśodā-devī
husband of Devakī

1.30.verse_text: The friendship of Yasoda is described in the following statement of the Adi Purana : "The wife of Maharaja Nanda was known by two names, Yasoda and Devaki. Partly because they share the same name (Devaki) the wife of Maharaja Nanda and the wife of Maharaja Vasudeva were great friends."

attributes: filled with a constantly rising flood of transcendental bliss
dearly loves her son Balarama
loves Krsna millions of times more than her own son
son of Rohini-devi who is dearly loved by her
loved by Rohini-devi millions of times more than her own son

1.31.verse_text: Lord Balarama's mother is named Rohini, because she is filled with a constantly rising (arohini) flood of transcendental bliss. Although she dearly loves her son Balarama, she loves Krsna millions of times more.

1.32.verse_text: Nanda Maharaja's elder brothers are Upananda and Abhinanda and his younger brothers are Sannanda and Nandana. These are the names of Lord Krsna's parental uncles.

wears garments the color of a cataka bird
is the dear wife of Upananda

1.33.verse_text: Upananda's complexion is pink. He has a long beard and wears green garments. Tungi-devi is his dear wife. Both her complexion and garments are the colour of a cataka bird.

attributes: wears dark garments and has a long beautiful beard like a great conchshell
wife of Abhinanda who wears blue garments and has a pink complexion

1.34.verse_text: Abhinanda wears dark garments and his long beautiful beard is like a great conchshell. Pivari-devi is his wife. She wears blue garments and her complexion is pink.

attributes: has a white complexion and wears dark-colored clothing
possesses two or three white hairs on his body
is very dear to Lord Keśava
known as Lord Keśava who holds Sannanda very dear

1.35.verse_text: Sannanda is also known as Sunanda. His complexion is white and he wears dark-colored clothing. He has two or three white hairs and he is very dear to Lord Kesava.

attributes: wife of Sannanda with a complexion the colour of a blue lotus flower
wears beautiful red garments
husband of Kuvalaya-devī
wears garments the color of a candata flower
has a complexion like the color of a peacock

1.36.verse_text: Kuvalaya-devi is Sannanda's wife. Her complexion is the colour of a blue lotus flower and she wears red garments. Nandana wears garments the color of a candata flower and his complexion is like the color of a peacock.

attributes: lives in the same house with his father Parjanya Maharaja

1.37.verse_text: Nandana lives in the same house with his father, Parjanya Maharaja. He is full of love for his yound nephew Krsna. Atulya-devi is Nandana's wife. Her complexion is like lightning and her garments resemble a dark cloud.

1.38.verse_text: Krsna's father has two sisters, Sananda-devi and Nandini-devi. They both dress in garments of many different colors. They have beautiful teeth and their complexions are the color of white foam. Sananda-devi's husband is Mahanila and Nandini's husband is Sunila.

1.39-40.verse_text: Kandava and Dandava are the two sons of Upananda. Their faces are as beautiful as lotus flowers and they become especially happy in the association of their friend Subala. Catu and Batuka are Nanda Maharaja's two ksatriya cousins born in Vasudeva Maharaja's dynasty. Catu's wife is Dadhisara and Batuka's wife is Havihsara.

attributes: energetic and enthusiastic maternal grandfather of Kṛṣṇa
has a long beard resembling a great conchshell
has a complexion the color of a ripe jambu fruit
grandson of Sumukha

1.41.verse_text: Krsna's energetic and enthusiastic maternal grandfather is named Sumukha. His long beard is like a great conchshell and his complexion is the color of a ripe jambu fruit.

1.42.verse_text: Queen Patala-devi is Krsna's maternal grandmother and she is very famous in Vrajabhumi. Her hair is the white color of yogurt, her complexion is the pink color of a patala flower and her garments are green.

dear friend of Mukhara-gopi

1.43.verse_text: Patala-devi's dear friend is named Mukhara-gopi. Out of intense affection for her friend, Mukhara used to feed the infant Yasoda with her own breast milk.

1.44-45.verse_text: Carumukha is Sumukha's younger brother. His complexion is the color of black eye-cosmetics and his wife Balaka-gopi was raised by her stepparents. Gola is the brother of Patala-devi, Krsna's maternal grandmother. His garments are the color of smoke. His brother-in-law, Sumukha, used to make fun of him and laugh and because of this Gola was very angry with him. Because he had worshiped Durvasa Muni in his previous birth, Gola was allowed to take birth in Vrajabhumi in the family of Ujjvala.

1.46.verse_text: Jatila-devi is Gola's wife. She has a large belly and her complexion is the color of a cow. Her sons Yasodhara, Yasodeva and Sudeva head the group of Krsna's maternal uncles.

1.47.verse_text: Krsna's maternal uncles have complexions like the color of a dark atasi flower and they dress in white garments. Their wives have complexions like karkati flowers and they dress in smoke-colored garments.

1.48.verse_text: Rema-devi, Roma-devi and Surema-devi are the daughters of Krsna's parental uncle Pavana. Yasodevi and Yasasvini are the sisters of Krsna's mother Yasoda. Yasasvini's husband is named Malla.

1.49.verse_text: Yasodevi is elder. Her complexion is dark and she is also known by the name Dadhisara. Yasasvini is younger. Her complexion is fair and she is also known by the name Havihsara. Both dress in vermillion-colored garments.

Among the priests, Vedagarbha, Mahayajva and Bhaguri are prominent. Their respective wives are Samadheni, Mahakavya and Vedika.

1.67-69.verse_text: Full of transcendental opulences, Paurnamasi-devi is the incarnation of the Lord's Yogamaya potency. She makes the arrangements of Lord Krsna's pastimes to be properly performed. She is slightly tall in stature and has a fair complexion. Her hair is the color of kasa flowers and she wears red garments. She is greatly respected by Nanda Maharaja and all the other residents of Vrajabhumi. She was the dear student of Devarsi Narada and on his advice she left her favorite son, Sandipani Muni, in Avantipura and came to Gokula, impelled by great love for her worshipable Lord Krsna.

attributes: has associates divided into two groups of gopis and gopas

1.70.verse_text: Krsna's associates may be divided into two groups : gopis and gopas. The gopis may be divided into three groups : 1. Gopi friends of the same age as Krsna, 2. maidservants and 3. gopi messengers.

attributes: possessor of associates divided into nine specific categories by learned scholars

1.71-72.verse_text: Learned scholars have also divided Lord Krsna's associates into the following nine categories : 1. yutha, 2. kula, 3. mandala, 4. varga, 5. gana, 6. samavaya, 7. sancaya, 8. samaja, and 9. and samanvaya.

attributes: lord who has three specific kinds of gopi associates
possessor of contemporary gopi friends who are most exalted

1.73-75.verse_text: Krsna's Gopi Friends : Of the three kinds of gopi associates of the Lord mentioned in text 70, the first group, Krsna's contemporary gopi friends, are the most exalted, the second group, the maidservants are the next most exalted, and the gopi messengers come after them. These three groups correspond to the groups mentioned as samaja, mandala and gana respectively (in texts 71 - 72). The first group, Krsna's contemporary gopi friends, may be further divided into varistha (the most exalted ) and vara (exalted).

attributes: more famous than all other gopis in Vraja
eternally the intimate friends of Sri Sri Radha-Krsna
possess a love for the divine couple that no one can equal or exceed
divine couple loved by the Varistha-gopis in an incomparable way

1.76.verse_text: Varistha (The Most Exalted Gopis): The varistha gopis are more famous than all the others. They are eternally the intimate friends of Sri Sri Radha-Krsna. No one can equal or exceed the love they bear for the divine couple.

attributes: the most worshipable of all of Kṛṣṇa's friends

1.77.verse_text: Of all Krsna's friends, the varistha gopis are the most worshipable. They are decorated with incomparable transcendental qualities, bodily beauty and all other charming opulences.

1.78.verse_text: Krsna's Gopi Friends: Lalita, Visakha, Citra, Campakamallika, Tungavidya, Indulekha, Rangadevi and Sudevi are the most intimate of Lord Krsna's gopi friends.

1.79.verse_text: Lalita: Of these eight gopis, the first one, Lalita-devi, is the best. She is a dear friend of the divine couple and she is 27 years old, the eldest of Krsna's gopi friends.

1.82-83.verse_text: Visakha: Visakha is the second most important of these eight varistha-gopis. Her attributes, activities and resolve are all muck like those of her friend Lalita. Visakha was born at the exact same moment as her dear friend Srimati Radharani appeared in this world. Visakha's garments are decorated with stars and her complexion is like lightning. Her father is Pavana, the son of the sister of Mukhara-gopi and her mother is Daksina-devi, the daughter of the sister of Jatila. Her husband is Vahika-gopa.

1.86.verse_text: Citra: Citra is the fourth of the varistha gopis. Her fair complexion resembles the color of kunkuma and her garments are the color of crystal. She is 26 days younger than Srimati Radharani. When Lord Madhava is full of blis, she becomes satisfied.

1.92.verse_text: Rangadevi: Rangadevi is the seventh of the varistha gopis. Her complexion is the color of a lotus filament and her garments are the color of a red rose. She is seven days younger than Srimati Radharani.

1.96-97.verse_text: The Vara-Gopis: Just as there are eight varistha (most exalted) gopis, the same way there are also eight vara (exalted) gopis. The vara-gopis are all teenage girls. Their names are Kalavati, Subhangada, Hiranyangi, Ratnalekha, Sikhavati, Kandarpa manjari, Phullakalika and Ananga manjari

elder brother of Kapota

1.99.verse_text: Her complexion is the color of yellow sandalwood and she wears garments the color of a parrot. Her husband is Kapota, the youngest brother of Vahika.

1.100.verse_text: Subhangada: Subhangada is the younger sister of Visakha. She is a very fair complexion and is married to Patatri, the younger brother of Pithara.

1.101.verse_text: Hiranyangi: Hiranyangi's complexion is the color of gold and she appears to be a temple or palace in which all beauty is conserved. She was born from the womb of Harini-devi.

engaged Bhaguri Muni in performing a Vedic sacrifice to attain his desires

1.103.verse_text: Mahavasu-gopa desired to beget a powerful and heroic son and a beautiful daughter. To attain this end the self-controlled Mahavasu engaged Bhaguri Muni in performing a Vedic sacrifice.

attributes: delighted personality who participated in the sacrifice
gave nectarean foodstuffs to his wife Sucandra-devī
wife of Mahāvasu who received nectarean foodstuffs from him

1.104.verse_text: Certain nectarean foodstuffs appeared from that sacrifice and the delighted Mahavasu gave them to his wife, Sucandra-devi.

attributes: gopi who was hastily eating sacred foodstuff on her front porch

1.105-106.verse_text: As Sucandra-devi was hastily eating the sacred foodstuff on her front porch, she spilled some of it. At that time the doe named Suranga, who was the mother of the doe named Rangini, was wandering in Vrajabhumi. Seeing Sucandra-devi spill some of the foodstuff, the doe Suranga quickly came forward and ate some of it. As a result of eating this sacred foodstuff, both the gopi Sucandra and the doe Suranga became pregnant.

attributes: mother of the son known to the gopas as Stokakṛṣṇa

1.107.verse_text: Sucandra-devi gave birth to a son whom the gopas called Stokakrsna and the deer gave birth to the girl Hiranyangi in the village of Vraja.

attributes: very dear friend to the beautiful Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī
dressed in beautiful garments appearing like a great multitude of unparalleled blossoming flowers
very dear to the beautiful Hiraṇyāṅgī

1.108.verse_text: Hiranyangi is very dear to Srimati Radharani and Srimati Radharani is very dear to her. Hiranyangi is dressed in beautiful garments that appear like a great multitude of unparalleled blossoming flowers.

attributes: father who promised his daughter in marriage with great respect
promised Hiranyangi to an elderly gopa who is completely unfit
exalted daughter promised in marriage by her father Mahavasu
intended bride of an elderly gopa completely unfit for her
elderly gopa who is completely unfit to become a husband

1.109.verse_text: With great respect the father Mahavasu then promised the exalted Hiranyangi in marriage to an elderly gopa who was completely unfit to become her husband.

1.110-111.verse_text: Ratnalekha: Maharaj Vrsabhanu's maternal cousin Payonidhi had a son but no daughter. His wife Mitra-devi desired to have a daughter also and to achieve this end she faithfully worshiped the sun-god, Vivasvan. By Vivasvan's mercy she gave birth to a daughter who was named Ratnalekha.

engaged in the devoted attentive worship of the sun-god
father of Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī
Lord Madhava who is seen and severely rebuked by Ratnalekha

1.112.verse_text: Ratnalekha's complexion is the red color of the mineral manahsila and her garments appear like a beautiful swarm of bumblebees. She is very dear to Srimati Radharani, the daughter of Maharaja Vrsabhanu. Her mother engages both her and her friend Radharani in the devoted attentive worship of the sun-god. When Ratnalekha sees Lord Madhava, her eyes begin to roll in ferocious anger and she severly rebukes Him.

attributes: has a complexion the color of a karṇikāra flower

1.113.verse_text: Sikhavati: Sikhavati's father is Dhanyadhanya and her mother is Susikha-devi. Sikhavati's complexion is the color of a karnikara flower. She is the younger sister of Kundalatika.

1.115-116.verse_text: Kandarpa-manjari: Kandarpa-manjari's father is Puspakara and her mother is Kuruvinda-devi. Kandarpa-manjari's father did not give her marriage to anyone, for he considered in his heart that Lord Hari is the only suitable husband for his daughter. Kandarpa-manjari's complexion is the color of a kinkirata bird and her garments are decorated with many different colors.

attributes: has a complexion dark like a blue lotus flower
wears garments that appear like a radiant rainbow
father of Phullakalikā
mother of Phullakalikā

1.117.verse_text: Phullakalika: Phullakalika's father is Srimalla and her mother is Kamalini. Phullakalika's complexion is dark like a blue lotus flower and her garments are like a rainbow.

attributes: has a loud voice capable of calling buffaloes from a great distance
has a forehead naturally marked with yellow tilaka lines

1.118.verse_text: Her forehead is naturally marked with yellow tilaka lines. Her husband, Vidura has a loud voice and is able to call the buffaloes from a great distance.

attributes: exquisitely beautiful personality named after Ananga the god of love
complexion resembling the color of a blooming springtime ketaki flower
dressed in garments with the shade of a blue lotus

1.119.verse_text: Ananga-manjari: Ananga-manjari is exquistely beautiful and therefore it is very appropriate that she is named after Ananga (cupid). Her complexion is the color of a springtime ketaki flower and her garments are the color of a blue lotus.

1.121.verse_text: General Description of the Activities of Krsna's Contemporary Gopi Friends: Krsna's contemporary gopi friends decorate their own dear friend Srimati Radharani. They cheat their husbands parents and other guardians (by going to meet Radha and Krsna) and they take Srimati Radharani's side when she has a lover's quarrel with Lord Hari.

1.122.verse_text: These gopis assist Radha and Krsna in Their secret rendezvous. These gopis serve palatable meals to the divine couple and afterward relish tasting the remnants left by Them. These gopis carefully guard the secret of Radha's and Krsna's confidential pastimes together.

attributes: rival of Candravali
served very appropriately by their pure-minded and intelligent associates
rival of Radharani whose followers are criticized by the associates of the divine couple

1.123.verse_text: Their minds are pure and they are very expert and intelligent. They serve Radha and Krsna very appropriately. They glorify their own group and criticize the followers of Candravali, Radharani's rival.

attributes: pleased by those who expertly sing, dance, and play music
served by those begging for engagement in various appropriate services
pleased by those who expertly sing, dance, and play music
served by those begging for engagement in various appropriate services

1.124.verse_text: They please Radha and Krsna by expertly singing, dancing and playing instrumental music. At the appropriate time they beg to be engaged in various appropriate services.

transcendental activities known by the learned devotees and the gopīs
possessor of gopī friends who actively serve Him

1.125.verse_text: For the most part the learned devotees know about these activities of Krsna's gopi friends. All the gopis are aware of these and all the other similar services and they actively serve Radha and Krsna by performing these activities.

attributes: divine couple served by gopis who are their close associates

1.126.verse_text: The gopis who are close associates of the divine couple are directly engaged in these services, whereas other gopis are not. We shall now describe, one by one, the gopis engaged in these intimate services.

attributes: leader and controller of the most exalted gopis
most dear to Śrī Kṛṣṇa among all the gopis
the one to whom the exalted gopis are most dear

1.127.verse_text: Among these most exalted gopis who are most dear to Krsna, the leader and controller is Lalita-devi.

couple whose meetings and conjugal struggles are arranged by Lalita
Lord Madhava who is sometimes offended by Lalita-devi

1.128.verse_text: Lalita-devi is full of ecstatic love for the divine couple. She is expert at arranging both Their meeting and Their conjugal struggle. Sometimes, for Radha's sake, she offends Lord Madhava.

attributes: beauty of all the other gopis is conserved in her form
mouth becomes bent with ferocious anger during an argument
expertly speaks the most outrageous and arrogant replies

1.129.verse_text: The beauty of all the other gopis appears to be conserved in the form of Lalita-devi. In an argument her mouth becomes bent with ferocious anger and she expertly speaks the most outrageous and arrogant replies.

takes the forefront in conflicts against Kṛṣṇa
audaciously stands a little away from the divine couple during their meetings

1.130.verse_text: When the arrogant gopis pick a quarrel with Krsna, she is in the forefront of the conflict. When Radha and Krsna meet, she audaciously remains standing a little away from them.

attributes: arranges for the meeting of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa with Paurṇamāsī
carries the parasol for the divine couple
decorates the divine couple with flowers
decorates the cottage where the divine couple rests and rises
assists in arranging the meeting of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa
recipients of the meeting arranged by Paurṇamāsī and Vṛndā
served by Vṛndā carrying a parasol
adorned with flowers by Vṛndā
resting at night and rising in a decorated cottage

1.131.verse_text: With the help of Purnamasi-devi and the other gopis she arranges for the meeting of Radha and Krsna. She carries the parasol for the divine couple, she decorates Them with flowers and she decorates the cottage where They rest at night and rise in the morning.

attributes: served by gopis in the garden known as Madanonmadini
provided with betel nuts by appointed gopis
garden filled with flowering creepers, betel creepers, and betel trees
location where gopis serve the divine couple
expert at playing various magic tricks
expert at composing riddles
appointed to provide betel nuts for the divine couple
servants of Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī
mistress served by various expert gopis

1.132-133.verse_text: Some gopis serve the divine couple in the garden known as Madanonmadini among the flowering creepers, betel creepers and betel trees. Some gopis are expert at playing various magic tricks, some are expert at composing riddles and some are appointed to provide betel nuts for the divine couple. All these gopis are the servants of Srimati Radharani.

attributes: leader and controller of all exalted and worshipable young gopis
exalted and worshipable young friends of Lord Baladeva
Lord Baladeva who is the friend of exalted young gopis

1.134.verse_text: Other gopis are specifically the friends of Lord Baladeva. Lalita-devi is the leader and controller of all these exalted and worshipable young gopis.

attributes: one of the dear vara-gopis who follows the footsteps of Lalita-devī
leader whose footsteps are followed by Ratnalekhā and eight other gopīs

1.135.verse_text: Ratnalekha and the eight other dear vara-gopis described here all follow the footsteps of Lalita-devi always and in all respects.

attributes: famous for her transcendental virtues and charming beauty
renowned for her expertise and charming sweetness
famous for her transcendental virtues and charming beauty
renowned for her expertise and charming sweetness

1.136.verse_text: Among these eight gopis Ratnaprabha-dvie and Ratikala-devi are famous for their transcendental virtues, beauty, experise and charming sweetness.

attributes: adorned with flower crowns and flowers in their hair
wearing flower earrings and flowers decorating their foreheads
decorated with flower necklaces, armlets, sashes, and bracelets
wearing flower anklets and flower bodices as personal ornaments
possess many other kinds of various beautiful flower ornaments
can be fashioned from precious jewels, gold, or other materials
may be made of various types of fragrant flowers

1.137-138.verse_text: Flower Decorations: The flower ornaments of the divine couple are 1. flower crown, 2. flowers in the hair, 3. flower earrings, 4. flowers decorating the forehead, 5. flower necklaces, 6. flower armlets, 7. flower sashes, 8. flower braelets, 9. flower anklets, 10. flower bodices and many other kinds of flower ornaments. Just as these ornaments may be fashioned from precious jewels, gold or other materials, in the same way they may be made of flowers.

attributes: adorned with crowns made of various fragrant flowers and precious gems
artistically constructed from rangini, yellow jasmine, navamali, and sumali flowers
decorated with dhrti gems, rubies, gomeda gems, pearls, and splendid moonstones

1.139-140.verse_text: Crown: The divine couple's crowns may be made of rangini flowers, yellow jasmine flowers, navamali flowers, sumali flowers, dhrti gems, rubies, gomeda gems, pearls or splendid moonstones. These may be artistically arranged to construct beautiful crowns.

attributes: crowns made with seven points of beautiful design
adorned with gold ketaki flowers and various flower buds
possesses colorful and beautiful ingredients that enchant the mind
enchanting ornament that captivates the mind of Lord Hari
Lord Hari whose mind is enchanted by the beautiful crown

1.141.verse_text: The crowns may be made with seven points and they may also have gold ketaki flowers or various flower buds among their colorful and beautiful ingredients. These crowns enchant the mind of Lord Hari.

attributes: flower crowns that are the best of all crown types
more pleasing than even the best jeweled crowns known as ratnapara
learned the art of making puṣpapara crowns from Rādhārāṇī
teacher who instructed Lalitā-devī in making puṣpapara crowns

1.142.verse_text: The flower crowns known as puspapara are the best of all and they are more pleasing than even the best jeweled crowns (ratnapara). Lalita-devi learned how to make these puspapara crowns from Srimati Radharani.

attributes: made with flowers and flower buds of five different colors
arranged in five points
especially used to decorate Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī
person who is especially decorated with the puṣpapara crown

1.143.verse_text: These puspapara crowns are made with flowers and flower buds of five different colors arranged in five points. This crown is especially used to decorate Srimati Radharani.

attributes: garland of flower buds and similar ingredients closely strung together
ornament traditionally placed on the hair

1.144.verse_text: Balapasya: A garland of flower buds and similar ingredients closely strung together and placed on the hair is called balapasya.

attributes: five distinct kinds known as tadanka, kundala, puspi, karnika and karna-vestana

1.145.verse_text: Earrings: Skilled craftsmen say there are five kinds of earrings. They are known by the names tadanka, kundala, puspi, karnika and karna-vestana.

attributes: hollow gold post worn in the ear
earring available in two distinct varieties
first variety features a golden ketaki flower petal attachment
second variety features a colorful flower petal attachment

1.146.verse_text: Tadanka: A hollow gold post worn in the ear is called a tadanka. This kind of earring is of two varieties. In the first variety the petal of golden ketaki flower is attached to this earring. In the second kind the petal of some other colorful flower is attached.

attributes: earring fashioned from flowers in order to resemble specific objects
exists in many different kinds of designs and shapes
flowers arranged to resemble peacocks, sharks, lotuses, or half-moons

1.147.verse_text: Kundala: When an earring is fashioned from flowers in order to resemble a certain object, the earring is called kundala. There are many different kinds of kundala earrings. The flowers may be arranged to resemble a peacock, shark, lotus, half-moon or many other things.

attributes: fashioned from four different kinds of flowers placed in a circle
possesses a large gunja berry situated in the middle

1.148.verse_text: Puspi: The puspi earrings is fashioned from four different kinds of flowers placed in a circle with a large gunja berry in the middle.

attributes: fashioned from the whorl of a blue lotus
surrounded by yellow flowers
contains a bhrngika flower and pomegranate flower in the middle

1.149.verse_text: Karnika: The karnika earring is fashioned from the whorl of a blue lotus surrounded by yellow flowers. In the middle are a bhrngika flower and pomegranate flower.

attributes: flower earring so large it completely covers the ear

1.150.verse_text: Karna-vestana: When the flower earring is so large it completely covers the ear, the earring is known as karna-vetsana.

attributes: garland of flowers placed on the upper forehead along the hairline
features flowers of two colors with red in the middle
has flowers of a different color on the two sides

1.151.verse_text: Decoration for the Forehead.: A garland of flowers placed on the upper forehead along the hairline is called lalatika. Such a garland should have flowers of two colors : red in the middle of the garland and the other color flowers on the two sides.

attributes: a necklace made of a single kind of flower
a floral ornament strung four times around the neck

1.152.verse_text: Graivekyaka (flower collar): Made of a single kind of flower, a necklace strung four times around the neck is called graiveyaka.

attributes: fashioned of three kinds of flowers strung one after another
resembles a little flower creeper

1.153.verse_text: Angada (armlet): A flower-armlet (angada) may be fashioned of three kinds of flowers strung one after another to resemble a little flower creeper.

attributes: sash made of flowers of five different colors
artistically strung together in a gently waving pattern

1.154.verse_text: Kanci (sash): A sash made of flowers of five different colors, artistically strung together in a gently waving pattern, is called a kanci.

attributes: anklets made of many different varieties of flower buds

1.155.verse_text: Kataka (flower anklets): Anklets made of many different flower buds are called kataka. These are of many different varieties.

attributes: a bracelet made of flowers of four colors
has bunches of flowers hanging down at three places

1.156.verse_text: Mani-bandhani (bracelet): A bracelet made of flowers of four colors, with bunches of flowers hanging down at three places, is called mani-bandhani.

attributes: decoration of flowers covering entire top and side parts of the feet
adorned with bunches of flowers in four places

1.157.verse_text: Hamsaka (flower shoes): When the decoration of flowers covers the entire top and side parts of the feet and there are bunches of flowers in four places, such a decoration is called hamsaka.

attributes: bodice made of flowers of six colors artistically arranged
perfumed with musk and beginning from the neck

1.158.verse_text: Kanculi (flower bodice): A bodice made of flowers of six colors, artistically arranged and perfumed with musk and which begins from the neck, is called kanculi.

attributes: parasol made of thin white sticks decorated with white flowers
has a handle decorated with yellow jasmine flowers

1.159.verse_text: Chatra (parasol): Made of thin white sticks, decorated with white flowers and with a handle decorated with yellow jasmine flowers, a flower parasol is called a chatra.

attributes: fashioned of fragrant campaka and malli flowers
decorated with delicate small bells that tinkle
has a large comfortable cushion of navamali flowers

1.160.verse_text: Sayanam (couch): The couch is fashioned of campaka and malli flowers and decorated with small bells. It has a large cushion of navamali flowers.

attributes: awning made of a latticework of various colorful flowers
constructed from ketaki petals and various types of leaves
adorned with many malli flowers hanging down from it

1.161.verse_text: Ulloca (an awning): An awning is made of a latticework of various colorful flowers, ketaki petals and various leaves. Many malli flowers hang down from this awning.

attributes: awning decorated with many sindhuvāra flowers as white as pearls on the sides
awning featuring fresh lotus flowers hanging down in the middle

1.162.verse_text: Candratapa (an awning): When many sindhuvara flowers as white as pearls decorate the sides and fresh lotus flowers hang down in the middle, the awning is called candratapa.

attributes: constructed with reeds acting as structural pillars
features a roof crafted from various colorful flowers
has four walls adorned with various colorful flowers

1.163.verse_text: Vesma (cottage): With reeds as pillars and various colorful flowers as the roof and four walls, a vesma (flower cottage) is constructed.

attributes: the most important among the exalted gopi messengers
expertly knows the geography of Vṛndāvana
intimately knows each grove and garden in Vṛndāvana
learned in the science of gardening
filled with great love for Śrī Śrī Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa
fair complexioned and dressed in colorful garments
one of the most important gopi messengers
expertly knows the geography of Vṛndāvana
intimately knows each grove and garden in Vṛndāvana
learned in the science of gardening
filled with great love for Śrī Śrī Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa
fair complexioned and dressed in colorful garments
one of the most important gopi messengers
expertly knows the geography of Vṛndāvana
intimately knows each grove and garden in Vṛndāvana
learned in the science of gardening
filled with great love for Śrī Śrī Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa
fair complexioned and dressed in colorful garments
one of the most important gopi messengers
expertly knows the geography of Vṛndāvana
intimately knows each grove and garden in Vṛndāvana
learned in the science of gardening
filled with great love for Śrī Śrī Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa
fair complexioned and dressed in colorful garments
the recipients of the great love of the gopi messengers

1.164a-b.verse_text: Gopi Messengers: Vrnda, Vrndarika, Mela and Murali are the most important gopi messengers. The gopi messengers expertly know the geograhy of Vrndavana and they intimately know each grove and garden there. They are also learned in the science of gardening. These exalted gopi messengers are all filled with great love for Sri Sri Radha and Krsna. The gopi messengers are fair complexioned and they dress in colorful garments. Vrnda-devi is the most important among them.

attributes: intimate friend of the divine couple
more exalted than other younger gopis
takes up the work of carrying messages between Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa
most intelligent and expert of all the gopi messengers
loquacious and expert at joking with Lord Govinda
perfect counsellor of the divine couple
expert at all aspects of amorous diplomacy
knows the arts of conciliating, bribing, and quarrelling with an angered lover
recipients of messages carried by Visākhā-devī
guided by their perfect counsellor Visākhā-devī
Lord Govinda who engages in joking with Visākhā-devī

1.165.verse_text: Visakha: Visakha-devi, the intimate friend of the divine couple, although she is more exalted than these younger gopis, also takes up the work of carrying messages between Radha and Krsna and she is the most intelligent and expert of all the gopi messengers. Loquacious Visakha is expert at joking with Lord Govinda and she is the perfect counsellor of the divine couple. She is expert at all aspects of amorous diplomacy and she knows all the arts of how to conciliate an angered lover, how to bribe him and how to quarrel with him.

attributes: expert at drawing various designs in tilaka
artistically strings beautiful flower garlands
composes clever verses and acrostics such as sarvatobhadra
conjures magical illusions by chanting sacred mantras
worships the sun god with various paraphernalia
sings in various foreign languages
composes different kinds of poetic verses

1.166.verse_text: Rangavali-devi and other gopis are expert at drawing various designs in tilaka, artistically stringing flower garlands, composing clever verses and acrostics such as sarvatobhadra, conjuring magical illusions by chanting mantras, worshipable the sun god with various paraphernalia, singing in various foreign languages and composing different kinds of poetry.

attributes: is dressed and decorated by the leaders of the gopi servants
a leader of the gopi servants who dress and decorate Srimati Radharani
a leader of the gopi servants who dress and decorate Srimati Radharani
a leader of the gopi servants who dress and decorate Srimati Radharani

1.167.verse_text: Maidservants who dress Srimati Radharani: Madhavai, Malati, and Candrarekha are the leaders the gopi servants who dress and decorate Srimati Radharani.

attributes: appointed the gopi messengers who have jurisdiction over flowering trees
leader of a group of gopi messengers
leader of the wonderfully blissful gopi messengers
member of the gopi messengers appointed by Vṛndā-devī
recognized as one of the groups of gopi messengers
third of the gopi messengers appointed to deliver messages
abode containing flowering trees under the jurisdiction of gopi messengers

1.168-169.verse_text: The gopi messengers appointed by Vrnda-devi have the flowering trees of Vrndavana under their jurisdiction. Malika-devi isthe leader of these wonderfully blissful gopis. In addition to these two groups of gopi messengers (1. Visakha and 2. the followers of Vrnda-devi), Campakalata-devi is the third of the gopi messengers.

attributes: veils her activities in great secrecy
expert at the art of logical persuasion
skilled diplomat who knows how to thwart Srimati Radharani's rivals
has rivals who are thwarted by the diplomacy of Campakalata

1.170.verse_text: Campakalata veils her activities in great secrecy. She is expert at the art of logical persuasion, and she is killed diplomat who knows how to thwart Srimati Radharani's rivals.

attributes: expert at collecting fruits, flowers, and roots from the forest
possesses the skill to artistically fashion things from clay using only her hands

1.171.verse_text: Campakalata is expert at collecting fruits, flowers, and roots from the forest. Using only the skill of her hands shecan artistically fashion things from clay.

attributes: expert cook who knows all literature describing the six flavors
expert at making various kinds of sweet candy
famous by the name Mistahasta meaning sweet hands

1.172.verse_text: Campakalata is an expert cook who knows all the literature describing the six flavors of gourmet cooking. Sheis so expert at making various kinds of candythat she has become famous by the name Mistahasta (sweet hands).

attributes: leader of the eight gopi maidservants expert at cooking
expert at cooking various preparations from Vraja milk products

1.173.verse_text: There are also eight gopi maidservants expert at cooking various preparations from the milk products Vraja village. Kurangaksi-devi is the leader of these gopi cooks.

attributes: leader of the gopis appointed as protectoresses of Vrndavana
protectoress of the trees, creepers, and bushes of Vrndavana
abode containing trees, creepers, and bushes protected by gopis

1.174.verse_text: Activities of the Eight Principal Gopies: Of all these gopis who are appointed as protectoresses of the trees, creepers, and bushes of Vrndavana, the leader is Campakalata-devi.

attributes: wonderfully expert in all the activities of the gopīs
especially expert in the lover's quarrel between Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa
participating in a lover's quarrel with Kṛṣṇa
participating in a lover's quarrel with Rādhārāṇī

1.175.verse_text: Citra: Citra-devi is wonderfully expert in all the activities wehavejust described. She is especially expert in the lover's quarrel between Radha and Krsna (the third of the six definitions of the word abhisarana).

attributes: can read between lines of books and letters written in many different languages
perceives the hidden intentions of an author through written work
skilled gourmet capable of understanding the tastes of various foods
understands the flavor of foods made with honey and milk simply by glancing

1.176.verse_text: Citra-devi can read between the lines of books and letters written in many different languages, perceiving the hidden intentions of the author. She is a skilled gourmet and can understand the testes of various foods made with honey, milk, and other ingredients simply by glancing at them.

attributes: especially expert at gardening and cultivating beautiful flower gardens
can nicely make various kinds of nectarean beverages

1.178.verse_text: She is especially expert at gardenting and she can nicely make various kinds of nectarean beverages.

attributes: leader of eight gopi maidservants in Vraja
expert at making various kinds of nectarean beverages

1.179.verse_text: There are also eight other gopi maidservants, headed by Rasalika-devi, who are expert at making various kinds of nectarean beverages.

attributes: the leader of the gopīs who collect medicinal herbs and creepers
mostly collect transcendental herbs and medicinal creepers from the forest
do not collect flowers or any other forest items

1.180.verse_text: There are other gopis who mostly collect transcendental herbs and medicinal creepers from the forest and do not collect flowers or anything else. Citra-devi is the leader of these gopis.

attributes: one of the leaders among the gopis
learned in the eighteen branches of knowledge

1.181.verse_text: Tungavidya: Tungavidya is one of the leaders of the gopis. She is learned in the eighteen branches of knowledge.

attributes: celebrated music teacher expert at playing the vina
expert at singing in the style known as marga

1.183.verse_text: She is a celebrated music teacher expert at playing the vina and singing in the style known as marga.

attributes: leader of eight gopī messengers expert in arranging political alliances
expert in the first of diplomatic maneuvers in the art of love
participant in political alliances arranged by expert gopī messengers
participant in political alliances arranged by expert gopī messengers

1.184.verse_text: Eight gopi messengers headed by Manjumedha-devi are especially expect at arranging political alliances (sandhi) the first of diplomatic maneuvers in the art of politics between Radha and Krsna.

attributes: the best of all dancers in Vraja
expert musicians at playing the mrdanga
singers of songs in various recital halls

1.185.verse_text: These gopis are the best of dancers. They are musicians expert at playing the mrdanga and singing in recital halls.

attributes: the leader of the gopis engaged in fetching water
especially engaged in fetching water from streams in Vṛndāvana
abode containing streams where gopis fetch water

1.186.verse_text: These gopis are especially engaged in fetching water from the streams in Vrndavana. Tungavidya is the leader of these gopis.

attributes: noble personality learned in the science and mantras of Naga-sastra
versed in the methods of charming snakes
learned in the Samudraka-sastra science of palmistry

1.187.verse_text: Indulekha: Noble Indulekha is learned in the science and mantras of the Naga-sastra, which describes various methods of charming snakes. She is also learned in the Samudraka-sastra, which describes the science of palmistry.

attributes: carries the auspicious messages of the divine couple in her hand
creates the good fortune of Radha and Krsna
creates the mutual love and attraction of the divine couple
recipients of auspicious messages delivered by Dhaniṣṭhā-devī
whose good fortune and mutual attraction are cultivated by Dhaniṣṭhā-devī

1.189.verse_text: In her hand she carries the auspicious messages of the divine couple. In this way she creates the good fortune of Radha and Krsna by creating Their mutual love and attraction.

attributes: leader of a group of gopīs who are friends and neighbours of Indulekhā
friend and neighbour to the group of gopīs headed by Tuṅgabhadrā-devī
leader of a gopī group that acts as messengers for the divine couple
recipients of messages delivered by the group of gopīs led by Palindhikā-devī

1.190.verse_text: The group of gopis headed by Tungabhadra-devi are the friends and neighours of Indulekha. Among these gopis is a group, headed by Palindhika-devi, which acts as messengers for the divine couple.

attributes: fully aware of the confidential secrets of the divine couple
recipients of ornaments and garments provided by Indulekha's friends
possessors of a treasury guarded by friends of Indulekha

1.191.verse_text: Indulekha-devi is fully aware of the confidential secrets of the divine couple. Some of her friends are engaged in providing ornaments for the divine couple, others provide exquisite garments and others guard the divine couple's treasury.

attributes: leader of all the gopis engaged in these services
engaged in services in the various parts of Vṛndāvana
gopis engaged in services in the various parts of Vṛndāvana
place where gopis are engaged in various services

1.192.verse_text: Indulekha-devi is thus the leader of all the gopis engaged in these services in the various parts of Vrndavana.

attributes: always like a great ocean of coquettish words and gestures
very fond of joking with her friend Srimati Radharani
friend of Ranga-devi who is joked with in Krishna's presence
Lord whose presence serves as the setting for Ranga-devi's joking

1.193.verse_text: Ranga-devi: Ranga-devi is always like a great ocean of coquettish words and gestures. She is very fond of joking with her friend Srimati Radharani in the presence of Lord Krishna.

attributes: expert in the fourth of the six diplomatic activities
expert logician who patiently waits for the enemy
attained a mantra to attract Lord Kṛṣṇa due to previous austerities
object of attraction through the mantra attained by Nandimukhī-devī

1.194.verse_text: Among the six activities of diplomacy she is especially expert in the fourth : patiently waiting for the enemy to make the next move. She is an expert logician and because of previous austerities she has attained a mantra by which she can attract Lord Krsna.

attributes: leader of the eight most important friends of Ranga-devi
served by a group of eight friends expert in perfumes and cosmetics

1.195.verse_text: Kalakanti-devi is the leader of the eight most important friends of Ranga-devi. These friends are all expert in the use of perfumes and cosmetics.

attributes: has friends who are expert in various personal services for the divine couple
served by fanning in the summer by the friends of Raṅga-devī

1.196.verse_text: Ranga-devi's friends are expert at burning aromatic incense, carrying coal during the winter and fanning the divine couple in the summer.

attributes: leader of all the gopis who are friends of Ranga-devi
gopis who are able to control the lions, deer, and other wild animals in the forest

1.197.verse_text: Ranga-devi's friends are able to control the lions, deer and other wild animals in the forest. Ranga-devi is the leader of all these gopis.

attributes: always remains at the side of her dear friend
arranges the hair of Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī
decorates the eyes of Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī with mascara
massages the body of Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī
dear friend who is served by Sudevī

1.198.verse_text: Sudevi: Sudevi always remains at the side of her dear friend Srimati Radharani. Sudevi arranges Radharani's hair, decorates Her eyes with mascara and massages Her body.

attributes: expert in training male and female parrots
expert in the pastimes of roosters
expert sailor who navigates with skill
fully aware of the auspicious and inauspicious omens described in the Sakuna-sastra

1.199.verse_text: She is expert in training male and female parrots and she is also expert in the pastimes of roosters. She is an expert sailor and she is fully aware of the auspicious and inauspicious omens described in the Sakuna-sastra.

attributes: expert at massaging the body with scented oils
knows how to start fires and keep them burning
knows which flowers blossom with the rising of the moon

1.200.verse_text: She is expert at massaging the body with scented oils, she knows how to start fires and keep them burning and she knows which flowers blossom with the rising of the moon.

attributes: expert at constructing leaf-spitoons and playing music on bells
expert at decorating couches in various ways
friend of Sudevī
friend of Kāverī-devī and others expert in decorative arts

1.201.verse_text: Kaveri-devi and the other friends of Sudevi are expert at constructing leaf-spitoons, playing music on bells and decorating couches in various ways.

attributes: leader of a group of gopī friends
entrusted with the decoration of the divine couple's sitting place
act as clever spies disguising themselves in various ways
move among Rādhārāṇī's rivals to discover their secrets
deities presiding over the Vṛndāvana forest
charged with the protection of the forest birds and bees
owners of the sitting place decorated by Sudevī's friends
has rivals including Candravalī and her friends
rival of Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī
forest overseen by the deities Sudevī's friends

1.202-203.verse_text: Sudevi's friends are also entrusted with the decoration of the divine couple's sitting place. Sudevi's friends act as clever spies, disguising themselves in various ways and moving among Radharani's rivals (Candravali and her friends) to discover their secrets. Sudevi's friends are the deities of Vrndavana forest and they are charged with the protection of the forest birds and bees. Sudevi is the leader of these gopis.

attributes: expert in wonderful arts, crafts and various transcendental duties

1.204.verse_text: Description of the Various Gopis: The wonderful arts, crafts and other duties that comprise the activities of the gopis will now be described.

attributes: leader among the gopis who are strong and stubborn in arguments
dresses in beautiful red garments
very beautiful personality
embarrasses Kṛṣṇa by attacking Him with many ferocious witty puns
is embarrassed by Pindaka-devi through attacks of ferocious witty puns
group of gopis who are very strong and stubborn when there is conflict
includes Pindaka-devi, Nirvitandika-devi, Pundarika-devi, Sitakhandi-devi, Carucandi-devi, Sudantika-devi, Akunthita-devi, Kalakanthi-devi, Ramaci-devi, and Mecika-devi

1.205-206.verse_text: Pindaka-devi, Nirvitandika-devi, Pundarika-devi, Sitakhandi-devi, Carucandi-devi, Sudantika-devi, Akunthita-devi, Kalakanthi-devi, Ramaci-devi and Mecika-devi are among the gopis who are very strong and stubborn when there is an argument or conflict. Among them Pindaka-devi is the leader. She dresses in red garments. She is very beautiful. When Lord Krsna comes she embarrasses Him by attacking Him with many ferocious witty puns.

attributes: speaks many illogical and frivolous objections
leads Lord Kṛṣṇa to the place where Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī waits
speaks many illogical and frivolous objections

1.207.verse_text: Haridrabha-devi, Hariccela-devi and Harimitra-devi speak many illogical and frivolous objections as they lead Lord Krsna to the place where Srimati Radharani waits for Him.

attributes: garments and complexion are the color of a white lotus flower
ferociously mocks the lotus-eyed Lord Kṛṣṇa
lotus-eyed Lord who is mocked by Puṇḍarīka-devī

1.208.verse_text: Pundarika-devi's garments and complexion are both the color of a white lotus flower. She ferociously mocks lotus-eyed Lord Krsna.

has a bodily complexion the color of a peacock
possesses sweet and charming words often laced with acid sarcasms
jokingly calls Gaurī-devī by the name Sitakhandi

1.209.verse_text: Gauri-devi always wears white garments. Her complexion is the color of a peacock. Because her sweet and charming words are often laced with acid sarcasms, Lord Krsna jokingly calls her Sitakhandi (always sweet).

attributes: has words that are sometimes mild and pleasant
has words that are sometimes harsh and violent
complexion is the color of a black bee
garments are the color of lightning

1.210.verse_text: Gauri-devi's sister is known as Carucandi because her words are sometimes mild and pleasant (caru) and sometimes harsh and violent (candi). Carucandi's complexion is the color of a black bee and her garments are the color of lightning.

attributes: wears garments the color of a kurunthaka flower
possesses a complexion the color of a śirīṣa flower
expert at inflaming the amorous sentiments of ujjvala-rasa

1.211.verse_text: Sudantika-devi wears garments the color of a kurunthaka flower. Her complexion is the color of a sirisa flower. She is expert at inflaming the amorous sentiments of ujjvala-rasa.

1.212.verse_text: Akunthita-devi's complexion is the color of a lotus stem. She wears white garments the color of the fibers within a lotus stem. She likes to insult Krsna for the amusement of her gopi friends.

1.213.verse_text: Kalakanthi-devi's complexion is the color of a kuli flower and her garments are the color of milk. She speaks to Lord Krnsa, describing Radharani's jealous anger and advising Him to beg forgiveness from Her.

1.214.verse_text: Ramaci-devi's is the daughter of Lalita-devi's nurse. Ramaci's complexion is golden and her garments are the color of a parrot. She takes pleasure in jokingly insulting Krsna and laughing at Him.

1.215.verse_text: Ramaci-devi always wears white garments. Her complexion is the color of a pinda flower. She is expert at insulting Lord Krnsa.

1.216-217.verse_text: Petari-devi, Varuda-devi, Cari-devi, Kotari-devi, Kalatippani-devi, Marunda-devi, Morata-devi, Cuda-devi, Cundari-devi and Gondika-devi are the leaders of those gopi messengers who are past the prime of youth. These older gopis can argue with great stubbornness and they can also nicely sing as the divine couple take their meal. These gopis are always engaged in making arrangements for the forest pastimes of the divine couple.

attributes: elderly Gujarati lady whose hair color resembles fibers within a lotus stem
native of Garuda-desa whose braided hair resembles the current of a river

1.218.verse_text: Petari-devi is an elderly Gujarati lady whose hair is the color of fibers within a lotus stem. Varudi-devi is a native of Garuda-desa and her braided hair is like the current of a river.

performed severe austerities and took shelter of goddess Katyayani
sister of Kucari-devi
sister of Cari-devi

1.219.verse_text: Cari-devi, who is Kucari-devi's siste, is also known by the name Tapahkatyayani, because she performed severe austerities (tapah) and took shelter of goddess Katyayani (Durga). Kotari-devi was born in the abhira caste. Her hair is a mixture of black and white resembling rice mixed with toasted sesame seeds.

attributes: elderly washerwoman with white hair
elderly woman with white eyebrows and a shaved head

1.220.verse_text: Kalatippani-devi is an elderly washerwoman with white hair. Marunda-devi has white eyebrows and a shaved head.

attributes: agile and has hair the color of a kāśa flower
possesses a wrinkled face
forehead is decorated with many grey hairs

1.221.verse_text: Agile Morata-devi has hair the color of a kasa flower. Cuda-devi has a wrinkled face and a forehead decorated with many grey hairs.

attributes: brahmani who is not as old as the others
lotus-eyed Lord Kṛṣṇa glorifies her and treats her with great respect
lotus-eyed Lord who glorifies and respects Cundari-devi
shaves the splendid white hairs on the head of Cundari-devi
has cheeks that are wrinkled with age

1.222.verse_text: The brahmani Cundari-devi is not as old as the others. Lotus-eyed Lord Krsna glorifies her and treats her with great respect. Gondika-devi shaves the splendid white hairs on her head. Her cheeks are wrinkled with age.

1.223-224.verse_text: Gopi Messengers who Arrange the Meeting of the Divine Couple: Sivada-devi, Saumyadarsana-devi, Suprasada-devi, Sadasanta-devi, Santida-devi and Kantida-devi are the leaders of the gopi messengers who expertly arrange the meeting of Radha and Krsna. These gopis consider Lalita-devi to be their dearmost life and soul. They are counted among the intimate associates of Lord Krsna.

quarrelled with Srimati Radharani

1.225.verse_text: At a certain time Srimati Radharani quarrels with Lord Krsna and refuses to see Him. Understanding the hints of Lalita-devi, these gopis then approach Lord Krsna.

attributes: becomes very pleased upon receiving the peace offering from Kṛṣṇa
grants Her mercy to the gopīs who act as messengers
is appeased and pleased by the gopīs in different ways
is convinced by the gopīs that His actual desire is to meet Rādhā
gives a peace offering to the gopīs to present to Rādhārāṇī

recipients of the mercy of Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī

1.226-227.verse_text: These gopis appease Lord Krsna and please Him in different ways. With great effort they convince Him that His actual desire is to meet Radha again. When These gopis bring to Her the gift that Lord Krsna gave them as a peace offering, Srimati Radharani becomes very pleased with them and grants them Her mercy.

1.228-229.verse_text: Sivada-devi was born in the Ragyhu dynasty, Saumyadarsana-devi in the dynasty of the moon-god, Suprasada-devi in the Puru dynasty, Sadasanta-devi in a family of ascetics and Santida and Kantida in brahmana families. By the mercy of Narada Muni they were all able to reside in Vraja.

attributes: recipients of love from the second group of gopīs

1.230.verse_text: The Second Gruop of Gopis: After this first group of gopis there is a second group of gopis, whose love for the divine couple is a little less than that of the first group. This second group of gopis may again be divided into two groups : sama-prema and asama-prema. These gopis will be described in the following verses.

attributes: divided into two groups of eternally perfect and perfected by service

1.231.verse_text: The sama-prema gopis may be divided into two groups - those who are eternally perfect and those who have attained perfection by engaging in devotional service.

attributes: eight hundred thousand out of one hundred million are eternally perfect

1.232.verse_text: Out of one hundred million gopis, eight hundred thousand are eternally perfect.

1.233-234.verse_text: The direct followers of the eight principal gopis are counted in different ways. Some say they number five thousand, others say they number four or five thousand, still others say they number three or four thousand and still others say they number one thousand.

attributes: the divine couple whose nature of love defines groups of gopi followers

1.235.verse_text: Some say the followers of the eight principal gopis are divided into many groups and others say they are not divided into groups at all. Some say they are divided into sixteen groups according to the nature of their love for the divine couple.

attributes: organized into various groups of twenty, twenty-five, thirty, sixty, or sixty-four

1.236.verse_text: Some say these gopis are divided into twenty groups, some say there are twenty-five groups, some say there are thirty, some say there are sixty and some say there are sixty-four groups of gopis.

1.237.verse_text: Some say this group of gopis is in turn divided into two subgroups. Others say it is divided into two or three subgroups and others say it is divided into three or four subgroups.

attributes: entire community divided into forty or five hundred different groups

1.238.verse_text: The entire gopi community may be divided in different ways. Some say there are forty groups of gopis and others that there are five hundred groups.

1.239-246.verse_text: Aside from the eight varistha-gopas and the eight vara-gopis, sixty four gopis are considered most important. Their names are : 1. Ratnaprabha, 2. Ratikala, 3. Subhadra, 4. Ratika, 5. Sumukhi, 6. Dhanistha, 7. Kalahamsi, 8. Kalapini, 9. Madhavi, 10. Malati, 11. Candrarekha, 12. Kunjari, 13. Harini, 14. Capala, 15. Damni, 16. Surabhi, 17. Subhanana, 18. Kurangaksi, 19. Sucarita, 20. Mandali, 21. Manikundala, 22. Candrika, 23. Candralatika, 24. Pankajaksi, 25. Sumandira, 26. Rasalika, 27. Tilakini, 28. Sauraseni, 29. Sugandhika, 30. Ramani, 31. Kamanagari, 32. Nagari, 33. Nagavenika, 34. Manjumedha, 35. Sumadhura, 36. Sumadhya, 37. Madhureksana, 38. Tanumadhya, 39. Madhuspanda, 40. Gunacuda, 41. Varangada, 42. Tungabhadra, 43. Rasottunga, 44. Rangavati, 45. Susangata, 46. Citrarekha, 47. Vicitrangi, 48. Modini, 49. Madanalasa, 50. Kalakanthi, 51. Sasikala, 52. Kamala, 53. Madhurendira, 54. Kandarpa-sundari, 55. Kamalatika, 56. Prema-manjari, 57. Kaveri, 58. Carukavara, 59. Sukesi, 60. Manjukesi, 61. Harahira, 62. Mahahira, 63. Harakanthi, 64. Manohara.

1.247.verse_text: The Sammohana-tantra's Description of Srimati Radharani's Eight Principal Friends: "Lilavati, Sadhika, Candrika, Madhavi, Lalita, Vijaya, Gauri and Nandi are the eight principal gopi friends of Srimati Radharani."

attributes: one of the eight principal gopis described in Vedic literature

1.248.verse_text: Another Description of the Eight Principal Gopis: Another quotation from Vedic literature gives the following description : "Kalavati, Srimati, Sudhamukhi, Visakha, Kaumudi, Madhvi and Sarada are the eight prinicpal gopis."

attributes: king and queen of Vṛndāvana with unlimited associates in the spiritual world
sacred realm where the king and queen possess unlimited associates

1.249-250.verse_text: The numbers presented in this description of the gopis has no actual relevance to the number of eternally liberated gopis in the spiritual world. There the associates of the king and queen of Vrndavana are unlimited in number. No one can count them. We have simply given some hints so the reader may understand the vast number of the associates of the divine couple.

attributes: recipients of services including preparation of couch, food, drink and betel nuts
swung on a swing by the gopis
decorated with tilaka by the gopis
perform many services for the divine couple including preparing their couch
provide food, drink, and betel nuts to the divine couple
swing the divine couple on a swing
decorate the divine couple with tilaka

1.251.verse_text: Preparing the divine couple's couch, food, drink and betel nuts, swinging Them on a swing and decorating Them with tilaka are among the many services performed by the gopis. The learned devotees may research this and enumerate all these services.

attributes: the subjects described within the various transcendental rasa-śāstras
effulgent sun who can remove the blindness from the eyes

1.252.verse_text: Rupa Goswami prays that the effulgent Krsna-sun may remove the blindness from his eyes and enable him to properly see and understand the various rasa-sastras that describe Sri Sri Radha-Krsna and Their associates.

attributes: the home of the king of Vraja
the king of Vraja who resides at Nandagrama

1.253.verse_text: In the year 1472 of the Saka era (1550 A.D.) on Sunday, the sixth day of the month of Sravana, at Nandagrama, the home of the king of Vraja, this book, the Radha-Krsna-ganoddesa-dipika was completed.

attributes: form glistens with the black mascara of the sweetness of His nectarean handsomeness
complexion is the color of a blue lotus flower or a sapphire

2.1.verse_text: Sri Krsna's Transcendental Form and Attributes: Sri Krsna's form glistens with the black mascara of the sweetness of His nectarean handsomeness. His complexion is the color of a blue lotus flower or a sapphire.

attributes: complexion as enchanting as an emerald, a tamala tree, or dark clouds
an ocean of nectarean handsomeness

2.2.verse_text: His complexion is as enchanting as an emerald, a tamala tree, or a group of beautiful dark clouds. He is an ocean of nectarean handsomeness.

attributes: wears yellow garments and a garland of forest flowers
decorated with various jewels
great reservoir of the nectar of many transcendental pastimes

2.3.verse_text: He wears yellow garments and a garland of forest flowers. He is decorated with various jewels and He is a great reservoir of the nectar of many transcendental pastimes.

attributes: possesses long curling hair anointed with many fragrant scents
wears a handsome crown decorated with many different flowers

2.4.verse_text: He has long, curling hair and He is anointed with many fragrant scents. His handsome crown is decorated with many different flowers.

attributes: handsome forehead splendidly decorated with tilaka markings and curling locks
possessor of playful movements of raised, dark eyebrows
enchanter of the hearts of the gopīs through facial expressions

2.5.verse_text: His handsome forehead is splendidly decorated with tilaka markings and curling locks of hair. The playful movements of His raised, dark eyebrows enchant the hearts of the gopis.

attributes: rolling eyes are as splendid as red and blue lotus flowers
tip of the nose is as handsome as the beak of Garuḍa

2.6.verse_text: His rolling eyes are as splendid as red and blue lotus flowers. The tip of His nose is as handsome as the beak of Garuda, the king of birds.

attributes: possesses charming ears and cheeks decorated with earrings made of various jewels

2.7.verse_text: His charming ears and cheeks are decorated with earrings made of various jewels.

attributes: possessor of a handsome lotus face splendid as millions of moons
speaks many charming and delightful jokes
possesses an exquisitely handsome and well-formed chin

2.8.verse_text: His handsome lotus face is as splendid as millions of moons. He speaks many charming jokes and His chin is exquisitely handsome.

attributes: handsome, smooth and charming neck that bends in three places
neck is decorated with a necklace of pearls
beauty of His neck enchants the residents of the three planetary systems

2.9.verse_text: His handsome, smooth and charming neck bends in three places. Decorated with a necklace of pearls, the beauty of His neck enchants the residents of the three planetary systems.

attributes: decorated with a necklace of pearls
has a chest adorned with the Kaustubha gem shining like lightning
possesses a handsome chest longing to enjoy the company of beautiful gopis

2.10.verse_text: Decorated with a necklace of pearls and with the Kaustubha gem, which shines like lightning, Krsna's handsome chest longs to enjoy the company of the beautiful gopis.

attributes: arms decorated with bracelets and armlets hanging down to His knees
has reddish lotus hands decorated with various auspicious signs

2.11.verse_text: Decorated with bracelets and armlets, Krsna's arms hang down to His knees. His reddish lotus hands are decorated with various auspicious signs.

attributes: hands beautifully decorated with auspicious marks
possessor of divine symbols including club, conchshell, barleycorn, and parasol
possessor of divine marks of half-moon, elephant rod, flag, and lotus
possessor of divine signs of sacrificial post, plow, pitcher, and fish

2.12.verse_text: Krsna's hands are beautifully decorated with the auspicious signs of a club, conchshell, barleycorn, parasol, half-moon, rod for controlling elephants, flag, lotus flower, sacrificial post, plow, pitcher and fish.

attributes: charming abdomen serves as the pastime abode of handsomeness
possesses a nectarean back that longs for the touch of the beautiful gopis

2.13.verse_text: Krsna's charming abdomen is the pastime abode of handsomeness. His nectarean back seems to long for the playful touch of the beautiful gopis.

attributes: possesses hips like a nectarean lotus flower that bewilder the demigod Cupid
possesses thighs like two beautiful plantain trees that charm the hearts of all women

2.14.verse_text: The nectarean lotus flower that is Lord Krsna's hips bewilders the demigod Cupid. Krsna's thighs are like two beautiful plantain trees that charm the hearts of all women.

attributes: possessor of knees that are very splendid, charming, and handsome
possessor of charming lotus feet decorated with jeweled ankle-bells

2.15.verse_text: Krsna's knees are very splendid, charming and handsome. His charming lotus feet are decorated with jeweled ankle-bells.

attributes: possesses feet with the beautiful luster of blooming roses
feet decorated with various auspicious markings and symbols

2.16-17.verse_text: Krsna's feet have the luster of roses, and they are decorated with various auspicious markings, such as the markings of the disc, half-moon, octagon, triangle, barleycorn, sky, parasol, waterpot, conchshell, cow's hoofprint, svastika, rod for controlling elephants, lotus flower, bow and jambu fruit.

attributes: possesses handsome lotus feet like two oceans filled with happiness
has reddish toes decorated with the row of full moons that are His toenails

2.18.verse_text: Krsna's handsome lotus feet are like two oceans filled with the happiness of pure love. His reddish toes are decorated with the row fo full moons that are His toenails.

attributes: possessor of handsomeness that cannot be equalled by anything
transcendental form which arouses deep attraction in the reader

2.19.verse_text: Although we have sometimes compared Krsna's handsomeness to various things, nothing can actually be equal to it. In this place we have given a small indication of Krsna's handsomeness to arouse the attraction of the reader.

The leader of these well-wishing friends is a boy named Vijayaksa, whose mother, Ambika-devi, was Krsna's nurse. Ambika-devi worshiped goddess Parvati and performed great austerities to get a powerful son who could protect Krsna.
